title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Python Build Room
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds installable reproducible Bookbot package from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of installable reproducible Bookbot package while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Src Layout
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 4134 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-14babk" that uses case 1562 to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Installed Smoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Installed Smoke into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Installed Smoke, keep Installed Smoke connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-14babk
# Competency: bookbot-installed-smoke.
# Case 1562: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_installed_smoke_14babk(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14babk = "14babk-kbab41-1562"
    assert evidence_variant_14babk
    evidence_axes_14babk = (
        "corpus14babk",
        "policykbab41",
        "revision1562",
        "command14babk1562",
        "reportkbab411562",
        "test156214babk",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14babk)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
